name: Ulster (Ireland)

colors.symbolism: or, the gold field of the historic Ulster arms
the Red Hand of Ulster, the most distinctive provincial symbol
the silver cross of the de Burgh arms underlying the design

description: A gold field charged with a red heraldic cross, on the centre of which is a small white shield bearing the Red Hand of Ulster.
history: The flag of Ulster is the heraldic banner of the historic provincial arms, combining the cross of the de Burgh earls of Ulster with the Red Hand badge of the O'Neills (associated with the broader Uí Néill kindred). It is used to represent the historic nine-county province as a whole, distinct from the four-province flag of Northern Ireland.

features.role: cross of the Earldom of Ulster
features.symbolism: a red heraldic cross on the gold field, derived from the arms of the Norman de Burgh earls of Ulster.

an open right hand in red, the most enduring symbol of Ulster, traditionally associated with the Uí Néill and later with the O'Neill dynasty. Here borne on a small white inescutcheon at the centre of the cross.
